intent: Create an enterprise buyer and AI-platform standards crosswalk for SecurityRecipes so OWASP Agentic Top 10, NIST AI agent guidance, MCP authorization requirements, and frontier-lab prompt-injection defenses map directly to generated evidence packs, MCP tools, and runtime decision surfaces.

positioning.buyer: AI Platform, Product Security, AppSec, GRC, procurement security, model-risk, and acquisition diligence teams
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes can answer standards and due-diligence questions with current source references, mapped controls, generated JSON evidence, and MCP tools instead of narrative claims.
positioning.why_now: Agentic AI systems now combine autonomy, private context, tool execution, remote MCP servers, local STDIO servers, browser-like agents, persistent memory, and agent-to-agent handoffs. Standards and frontier-lab guidance are converging on the same requirement: prove context, authority, tool use, egress, evals, and receipts before agents act.

buyer_success_criteria: Every mapped standard control has at least three SecurityRecipes capabilities.
Every mapped capability exposes generated JSON or source-controlled evidence.
Every high-risk agentic topic maps to at least one MCP tool or deterministic evaluator.
Every diligence answer links standards guidance to the secure context layer rather than only to prompt wording.
The generated pack can be regenerated in CI after standards, content, or evidence changes.

source_references.name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
source_references.why_it_matters: Peer-reviewed agentic guidance now gives buyers a shared language for autonomous agent risks such as goal hijack, tool misuse, identity abuse, memory poisoning, inter-agent communication, cascading failures, and rogue behavior.

source_references.name: Agentic AI Threats and Mitigations
source_references.why_it_matters: Threat-model-based OWASP guidance reinforces that agent autonomy, retrieved context, tool invocation, and delegation require controls beyond classic LLM application hardening.

source_references.name: Securing Agentic Applications Guide 1.0
source_references.why_it_matters: Practical design and deployment guidance supports translating agentic threats into concrete build, test, deployment, and operations controls.

source_references.name: OWASP GenAI Exploit Round-up Report Q1 2026
source_references.why_it_matters: Recent exploit reporting ties agent tooling, source leaks, MCP integrations, supply-chain abuse, and RCE patterns to operational mitigations.

source_references.name: AI Agent Standards Initiative
source_references.why_it_matters: NIST is prioritizing interoperable agent standards, community-led protocols, agent authentication, identity infrastructure, and security evaluations for human-agent and multi-agent interactions.

source_references.name: CAISI Request for Information on Securing AI Agent Systems
source_references.why_it_matters: CAISI highlights distinct agent risks from model outputs combined with software functionality, including indirect prompt injection, poisoning, misaligned actions, and deployment access controls.

source_references.name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework
source_references.why_it_matters: NIST continues expanding AI RMF work, including a 2026 critical-infrastructure profile concept note, reinforcing governance, lifecycle risk management, measurement, and operational monitoring.

source_references.name: Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework: Generative Artificial Intelligence Profile
source_references.why_it_matters: The NIST GenAI profile anchors provenance, third-party component risk, data governance, monitoring, evaluation, and lifecycle risk treatment for deployed generative AI systems.

source_references.name: Model Context Protocol Authorization Specification
source_references.why_it_matters: The current MCP authorization specification requires protected resource metadata, OAuth 2.1 alignment, resource indicators, token audience validation, PKCE support, and token-passthrough denial for protected MCP servers.

source_references.name: Model Context Protocol Security Best Practices
source_references.why_it_matters: MCP guidance covers confused deputy attacks, token passthrough, SSRF, session safety, local-server sandboxing, scope minimization, auditability, and least-privilege access.

source_references.name: Understanding prompt injections: a frontier security challenge
source_references.why_it_matters: OpenAI frames prompt injection as a social-engineering risk that grows as agents access sensitive data and take actions through tools and connected apps.

source_references.name: Designing AI agents to resist prompt injection
source_references.why_it_matters: OpenAI notes that effective prompt-injection attacks increasingly resemble social engineering, so defenses need egress checks, user confirmation, data controls, and layered safeguards beyond string filtering.

source_references.name: Mitigating the risk of prompt injections in browser use
source_references.why_it_matters: Anthropic emphasizes that browser agents operate in an adversarial web environment and need untrusted-content classifiers, model safeguards, red teaming, and ongoing hardening.

source_references.name: STAR for AI Catastrophic Risk Annex
source_references.why_it_matters: CSA's 2026 catastrophic-risk work makes loss of human oversight, uncontrolled system behavior, and irreversible large-scale consequences explicit buyer and auditor concerns for agentic AI.

capabilities.title: Agentic Standards Crosswalk
capabilities.commercial_value: Turns standards diligence into a compact MCP-readable product artifact for procurement, platform review, and acquisition diligence.

capabilities.title: Agentic Threat Radar
capabilities.commercial_value: Keeps the roadmap anchored to current agentic AI and MCP security guidance.

capabilities.title: Agentic Control Plane Blueprint
capabilities.commercial_value: Packages the secure context layer into a buyer-grade architecture and commercialization story.

capabilities.title: Agentic Catastrophic Risk Annex
capabilities.commercial_value: Adds board-grade severe-risk assurance for high-impact agent actions, runtime kill signals, approval receipts, and risk acceptance.

capabilities.title: Enterprise Trust Center Export
capabilities.commercial_value: Bundles generated evidence into one trust-center packet for procurement and acquirer diligence.

capabilities.title: Agentic Assurance Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Maps workflow, policy, and evidence controls to enterprise AI assurance requirements.

capabilities.title: Agentic Readiness Scorecard
capabilities.commercial_value: Turns scale decisions into evidence-backed pilot, gate, block, or scale outcomes.

capabilities.title: Agentic Red-Team Drill Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Provides adversarial replay scenarios for prompt injection, tool misuse, connector drift, and evidence failures.

capabilities.title: Agentic Measurement Probe Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Measures traceability across context, identity, MCP authorization, egress, red-team, readiness, and receipts.

capabilities.title: Agentic System BOM
capabilities.commercial_value: Inventories workflows, agents, identities, MCP connectors, evidence artifacts, evals, and drift triggers.

capabilities.title: Agentic Run Receipt Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Creates portable run proof for identity, context, tools, egress, approvals, verifiers, evidence, and revocation.

capabilities.title: Workflow Control Plane
capabilities.commercial_value: Declares workflow ownership, scope, gates, evidence, KPIs, and kill signals.

capabilities.title: MCP Gateway Policy
capabilities.commercial_value: Enforces default-deny MCP tool access, approval holds, scoped branch writes, and runtime kill decisions.

capabilities.title: MCP Authorization Conformance
capabilities.commercial_value: Checks resource indicators, token audience, PKCE, token passthrough, session binding, and scope drift.

capabilities.title: MCP Connector Trust Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Inventories MCP namespaces, owners, trust tiers, allowed access modes, promotion criteria, and kill signals.

capabilities.title: MCP Connector Intake Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Scores new or changed MCP servers before connector promotion.

capabilities.title: MCP STDIO Launch Boundary
capabilities.commercial_value: Controls local MCP server launch, sandboxing, command allowlists, package-install-on-launch, env exposure, and secret/network kill rules.

capabilities.title: Agent Identity and Delegation Ledger
capabilities.commercial_value: Defines non-human identity contracts, delegated MCP scopes, explicit denies, owners, and revocation triggers.

capabilities.title: Agent Capability Risk Register
capabilities.commercial_value: Scores workflow residual risk from autonomy, permissions, system criticality, impact radius, and control credits.

capabilities.title: Agent Memory Boundary Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Controls persistent memory, vector recall, TTL, provenance, rollback, tenant state, and prohibited persistence.

capabilities.title: Agent Skill Supply-Chain Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Governs skills, hooks, rules, extensions, permissions, package hashes, signatures, sandboxing, and network egress.

capabilities.title: Secure Context Trust Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Proves which context roots agents can retrieve, who owns them, how fresh they are, and how source hashes are bound.

capabilities.title: Secure Context Attestation Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Turns context sources and workflow packages into hash-verifiable attestation subjects.

capabilities.title: Secure Context Eval Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Tests retrieval correctness, attestation holds, poisoning resilience, egress safety, citations, and handoff boundaries.

capabilities.title: Context Poisoning Guard Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Scans registered context for prompt-injection, tool-poisoning, approval-bypass, hidden-instruction, encoded payload, and exfiltration markers.

capabilities.title: Context Egress Boundary Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Decides whether context can leave a tenant, model provider, MCP server, telemetry sink, public corpus, or external URL.

capabilities.title: Agent Handoff Boundary Pack
capabilities.commercial_value: Controls MCP, A2A, provider-native, and approval-bridge handoffs before context or authority crosses agent boundaries.

standards.title: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
standards.buyer_question: Can SecurityRecipes map every major agentic application risk to concrete evidence, runtime checks, and MCP tools?

controls.title: Agent Goal Hijacking
controls.objective: Prevent untrusted context or tool output from overriding the user's authorized objective.
controls.diligence_question: Which controls prove that hostile context is treated as evidence, not instruction?
controls.evidence_expectation: Poisoning scan results, eval scenarios, red-team drills, and gateway decisions show context demotion and default-deny behavior.

controls.title: Tool Misuse and Exploitation
controls.objective: Constrain legitimate tools so an agent cannot use them outside approved workflow, namespace, or access mode.
controls.diligence_question: What stops an agent from misusing an approved MCP tool or local server?
controls.evidence_expectation: Gateway policy, authorization conformance, connector trust, and launch-boundary evidence define allowed and prohibited tool use.

controls.title: Identity and Privilege Abuse
controls.objective: Bind agent runs to scoped, revocable non-human identities instead of implicit user authority.
controls.diligence_question: Which identity acted, what was delegated, and how can one unsafe run be revoked?
controls.evidence_expectation: Identity ledger, authorization, capability risk, and run receipt evidence show authority scope and revocation triggers.

controls.title: Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
controls.objective: Govern skills, rules, hooks, local MCP servers, connector schemas, and agent behavior packages as supply-chain artifacts.
controls.diligence_question: How are instructions, skills, hooks, and connector changes reviewed before they inherit authority?
controls.evidence_expectation: Skill, connector, trust registry, and BOM evidence expose provenance, permissions, hashes, and promotion status.

controls.title: Unexpected Code Execution
controls.objective: Block command, sandbox, package-install, and code-execution paths that do not match the approved workflow.
controls.diligence_question: What prevents config, tool metadata, or a skill from becoming an unreviewed code execution path?
controls.evidence_expectation: Launch-boundary, gateway, skill, and red-team evidence define command allowlists, sandboxing, and kill-session triggers.

controls.title: Memory and Context Poisoning
controls.objective: Prevent persistent memory, vector recall, and retrieved context from carrying hidden instructions or stale authority.
controls.diligence_question: How are memory and retrieved context separated from trusted instruction and recertified over time?
controls.evidence_expectation: Memory class policy, poisoning scan, trust pack, and attestation evidence show TTLs, provenance, source hashes, and holds.

controls.title: Insecure Inter-Agent Communication
controls.objective: Minimize and authenticate context crossing MCP, A2A, provider-native, and approval-bridge handoffs.
controls.diligence_question: Can a remote or delegated agent receive only allowed metadata and cited evidence?
controls.evidence_expectation: Handoff, egress, authorization, and identity evidence define protocol trust, payload fields, data classes, and approval holds.

controls.title: Cascading Failures
controls.objective: Detect loops, drift, failed verifiers, connector changes, and unsafe scale conditions before one run affects many systems.
controls.diligence_question: Which generated evidence prevents a small failure from becoming a fleet-wide failure?
controls.evidence_expectation: Readiness, probes, receipts, and drills show scale gates, replay cases, verifier requirements, and closure criteria.

controls.title: Human-Agent Trust Exploitation
controls.objective: Make approval, review, and evidence structured so users are not tricked into approving the wrong action.
controls.diligence_question: How does SecurityRecipes prove that a human approval matches the actual tool, data, and egress request?
controls.evidence_expectation: Receipts, gateway holds, red-team scenarios, and eval contracts make approval prompts auditable and replayable.

controls.title: Rogue Agents
controls.objective: Terminate agents that exceed scope, request prohibited data, attempt release actions, or keep operating after kill signals.
controls.diligence_question: What kills an unsafe run and how is that decision recorded?
controls.evidence_expectation: Gateway policy, identity revocation, run receipt, and readiness evidence expose kill-session criteria and post-run state.

standards.title: MCP Authorization and Security Best Practices
standards.buyer_question: Can protected MCP calls enforce identity, audience, resource, scope, sandbox, and session safety requirements before tool execution?

controls.title: Protected Resource Metadata and Discovery
controls.objective: Require MCP authorization decisions to be tied to known protected resources and reviewed connector metadata.
controls.diligence_question: How does a client know which authorization server and protected resource apply?
controls.evidence_expectation: Authorization conformance and connector intake evidence record resource indicators, connector IDs, namespaces, and trust state.

controls.title: Token Audience and Resource Binding
controls.objective: Reject tokens that were not issued for the MCP resource being called.
controls.diligence_question: Can SecurityRecipes prove a token was intended for this MCP server and operation?
controls.evidence_expectation: Authorization decisions, gateway policy, and receipts include audience, resource, scope, and correlation fields.

controls.title: Token Passthrough Forbidden
controls.objective: Prevent MCP servers from forwarding user or upstream tokens across trust boundaries.
controls.diligence_question: What blocks confused deputy and token-passthrough behavior?
controls.evidence_expectation: Authorization conformance denies passthrough, egress policy blocks secret movement, and connector trust records downstream boundaries.

controls.title: PKCE, HTTPS, and Secure Public Clients
controls.objective: Hold or deny protected MCP access when OAuth public-client safeguards are absent.
controls.diligence_question: How are public MCP clients and local launches treated before trust?
controls.evidence_expectation: Conformance, launch-boundary, and connector-intake artifacts show PKCE expectations, HTTPS requirements, and local launch controls.

controls.title: Scope Minimization and Step-Up
controls.objective: Start with least privilege and require explicit policy or approval before privileged tool access.
controls.diligence_question: How does a workflow avoid wildcard or omnibus scopes?
controls.evidence_expectation: Gateway, authorization, identity, and risk evidence map workflows to namespaces, access modes, scopes, and approval holds.

controls.title: Local Server Sandbox and STDIO Boundary
controls.objective: Treat local MCP server launch as a security boundary with command allowlists, sandboxing, and env isolation.
controls.diligence_question: What prevents local MCP servers from inheriting excessive filesystem, network, or secret access?
controls.evidence_expectation: STDIO launch, connector intake, and skill supply-chain packs record sandbox, command, package, env, and permission decisions.

standards.title: NIST AI Agent, AI RMF, CAISI, and GenAI Risk Guidance
standards.buyer_question: Can SecurityRecipes show governance, measurement, identity, data, and lifecycle evidence for deployed agent systems?

controls.title: Agent Identity and Interoperable Protocols
controls.objective: Support secure human-agent and multi-agent interactions through identity, authentication, and protocol-boundary evidence.
controls.diligence_question: Can the buyer inspect which agent identity acted across MCP and agent-to-agent boundaries?
controls.evidence_expectation: Handoff, identity, authorization, and BOM evidence show agent identity, protocol, capability, and resource boundaries.

controls.title: Security Evaluations and Measurement
controls.objective: Turn agent security evaluation into repeatable measurement and replay artifacts.
controls.diligence_question: Which evaluations must pass before a workflow scales?
controls.evidence_expectation: Eval, drill, probe, and readiness packs define scenarios, expected decisions, pass criteria, and promotion gates.

controls.title: Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage Lifecycle Risk
controls.objective: Make agentic workflows inspectable as governed systems with owners, intended use, evidence, metrics, residual risks, and review cadence.
controls.diligence_question: Can the product answer governance and lifecycle questions from one evidence surface?
controls.evidence_expectation: Assurance, blueprint, trust-center, and manifest artifacts expose owners, scope, evidence, controls, and residual risks.

controls.title: Constrain and Monitor Deployment Access
controls.objective: Constrain agent access in deployment and monitor tool, context, egress, and review decisions.
controls.diligence_question: What records prove deployment access was constrained and monitored?
controls.evidence_expectation: Gateway, receipt, identity, and egress evidence show decisions and correlation fields for each governed run.

controls.title: Data Provenance, Integrity, and Third-Party Risk
controls.objective: Track context provenance, source integrity, third-party connector boundaries, and data lifecycle controls.
controls.diligence_question: How does SecurityRecipes know which data and third-party components shaped an agent run?
controls.evidence_expectation: Trust, attestation, poisoning, and BOM evidence provide source hashes, owners, freshness, connector components, and drift triggers.

standards.title: Frontier-Lab Prompt Injection and Browser-Agent Defense Guidance
standards.buyer_question: Can SecurityRecipes translate frontier-lab prompt-injection guidance into controls that enterprises can run and inspect?

controls.title: Treat Indirect Prompt Injection as Social Engineering
controls.objective: Recognize that malicious instructions can arrive through normal-looking emails, web pages, documents, tickets, and tool outputs.
controls.diligence_question: How does the system detect manipulative context without relying only on brittle string filters?
controls.evidence_expectation: Poisoning rules, red-team scenarios, and eval cases cover hidden instructions, approval bypass, deceptive content, and malicious tool results.

controls.title: Block Silent Sensitive Data Transmission
controls.objective: Prevent agents from sending secrets, unredacted sensitive data, internal memory, or conversation-derived data to unapproved third parties.
controls.diligence_question: What stops data learned in context from being transmitted to a malicious URL, tool, or remote agent?
controls.evidence_expectation: Egress, receipts, gateway, and handoff evidence show destination class, data class, approvals, and kill-session decisions.

controls.title: Sandbox Tool and Code Execution
controls.objective: Isolate tools, local servers, and skills so prompt injection cannot produce harmful filesystem, network, package, or shell effects.
controls.diligence_question: How does the product keep tool execution bounded even when untrusted context reaches the agent?
controls.evidence_expectation: Launch, gateway, skill, and authorization decisions encode sandboxing, permission, access-mode, and token constraints.

controls.title: Continuous Hardening and Red-Team Loop
controls.objective: Refresh threat signals, probes, drills, and readiness after model, connector, prompt, or context changes.
controls.diligence_question: How does a buyer know the guidance has not gone stale after agent or model drift?
controls.evidence_expectation: Threat radar, drill, probe, and readiness packs create a regeneration loop tied to current source references.

controls.title: Minimize Broad Prompts, Connectors, and Autonomy
controls.objective: Avoid vague high-autonomy tasks by binding runs to explicit workflow, source, connector, and approval contracts.
controls.diligence_question: How does SecurityRecipes make AI easy without giving the agent broad, ambiguous authority?
controls.evidence_expectation: Identity, gateway, context, and authorization packs provide workflow-specific scope, source allowlists, connector permissions, and approval holds.

standards.title: CSA STAR for AI Catastrophic Risk Annex
standards.buyer_question: Can SecurityRecipes map severe-risk agentic AI concerns to testable controls, runtime decisions, and buyer-readable evidence?

controls.title: Prevent Loss of Human Oversight
controls.objective: Hold high-impact autonomous actions when approval, risk acceptance, identity, policy, authorization, or receipt evidence is missing.
controls.diligence_question: Which evidence fields must exist before a high-impact agent action can proceed?
controls.evidence_expectation: The catastrophic-risk annex, identity ledger, run receipts, and readiness scorecard show the approval record, risk acceptance, identity scope, policy hash, and runtime evidence required before high-impact autonomy.

controls.title: Kill Uncontrolled System Behavior
controls.objective: Terminate agent sessions when runtime behavior shows approval bypass, deny-after-deny looping, broad token requests, secret egress, or irreversible action without approval.
controls.diligence_question: What runtime signals override the agent's goal and force a kill-session decision?
controls.evidence_expectation: The annex, gateway policy, red-team drills, and measurement probes list kill signals, severe scenarios, expected decisions, and replay hooks.

controls.title: Gate Irreversible and Large-Scale Consequences
controls.objective: Deny or hold funds movement, production deployment, identity administration, mass deletion, public release, critical-infrastructure control, and connector scope escalation unless separate risk acceptance exists.
controls.diligence_question: How does the control plane separate ordinary remediation from irreversible or large-scale consequence domains?
controls.evidence_expectation: The annex, capability risk register, authorization conformance pack, and egress boundary identify high-impact action classes, residual risk, token scope constraints, and data-boundary decisions.

buyer_views.title: Procurement Security Review
buyer_views.question: Which standards and external references does SecurityRecipes track, and where is the evidence?
buyer_views.answer_contract: Return standards, controls, source references, evidence paths, and MCP tools with no customer-private data required.

buyer_views.title: AI Platform Architecture Review
buyer_views.question: Which controls must be enforced before agents can retrieve context, call MCP tools, delegate tasks, or persist memory?
buyer_views.answer_contract: Return the control-to-capability map, default-deny decisions, runtime evaluators, and generated pack paths for architecture review.

buyer_views.title: Acquisition Diligence
buyer_views.question: Why is SecurityRecipes a defensible control-plane asset rather than a static documentation site?
buyer_views.answer_contract: Return current source anchors, capability coverage, commercialization hooks, and evidence gaps in one MCP-readable packet.
